title: septimia streyi
intro: Septimia streyi, also known as "Tawa" or "Chilcotin rose", is a flowering plant native to the Chilcotin region of British Columbia, Canada. It has been used traditionally by the indigenous people for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Septimia streyi can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
